conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, let me review the official eligibility rules to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I can help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, **do you have any children that you're responsible for?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do! I have one child - Alex, who's 13 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now I need to ask you a few more questions about your situation with Alex.

**Do you live with Alex in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK and Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. 

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

**Is Alex currently in hospital, care, or any residential accommodation?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in hospital or care or anything like that. They're just at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for providing all that information. Based on what you've told me, I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:
- Alex is 13 years old, which is under 16, so meets the age requirement
- You live with Alex in the UK, which means you are responsible for them
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex (only one person can claim per child)
- Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation

You meet all the eligibility criteria and can proceed with claiming Child Benefit for Alex if you haven't already done so.

Please note: If you or your partner has an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay a High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but this doesn't affect your eligibility to claim.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 13 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 13 years old, which is under 16, so they meet the age requirement. You live with Alex in the UK, which means you are responsible for them. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, which is important as only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child. Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation, so there are no circumstances that would affect your entitlement. You meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 13-year-old child Alex. They live together in the UK, no one else is claiming for Alex, and there are no special circumstances (such as hospital or care) that would affect eligibility. All standard eligibility requirements are met.
